metadata.title: Rabi Oscillation — Spin Flopping Between Up and Down

beats.narration_text: Bonjour. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of rabi oscillation. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of rabi oscillation
output: ✓ Rabi Oscillation — Spin Flopping Between Up and Down

beats.narration_text: Rabi Oscillation is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. P↑(t) = cos²(ω₀t/2) — deterministic spin flip, no measurement, no collapse. NMR in one formula.. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Rabi Oscillation. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: Rabi freq ω₀ / (2π), Initial θ₀. Verify: P↑(t) = cos²(ω₀t/2) — deterministic spin flip, no measurement, no collapse. NMR in one formula.. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Rabi Oscillation. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: Rabi freq ω₀ / (2π), Initial θ₀. Verify: P↑(t) = cos²(

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.new_visual_element: Onda code-block: core JS (integrator + slider handler)
beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from qm2-rabi-oscillation.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: rabi oscillation. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag Rabi freq ω₀ / (2π) from 0.2 to 5. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of Rabi freq ω₀ / (2π) from 0.2 to 5

beats.narration_text: P↑(t) = cos²(ω₀t/2) — deterministic spin flip, no measurement, no collapse. NMR in one formula.. Exactly as the equations predict.
One prompt. A live demonstration of rabi oscillation. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Rabi Oscillation simulation and add a second slider that controls Rabi freq ω₀ / (2π). Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
